name: Ivern
playstyleSummary: Ivern is an unusual jungler who helps jungle camps instead of killing them, trading personal damage for powerful crowd control and utility for his team. He feels like playing a support who happens to live in the jungle, using roots, shields, and a giant sentinel to set up plays rather than dueling.
strengths: Strong crowd control with Rootcaller's root
Very safe early game since he can't be attacked by normal jungle camps
Great vision and ambush potential from Brushmaker
Powerful teamfight presence once he summons Daisy
weaknesses: Very low personal damage, especially early
Loses most 1v1 fights against real fighters
Mana hungry until he gets more items
Weak if caught alone without allies or Daisy

abilities.name: Friend of the Forest
abilities.howToUseIt: You can't damage or be damaged by normal jungle monsters, so grow groves on camps and free them once fully grown for gold and experience instead of auto-attacking them down.

abilities.name: Rootcaller
abilities.howToUseIt: Fire this at an enemy to root them, then follow up yourself or let an ally dash in to engage - it's your main tool for starting fights.

abilities.name: Brushmaker
abilities.howToUseIt: Activate it near a lane to plant a long-lasting patch of brush you can use to ambush enemies or set up wards; fighting inside any brush also boosts your and nearby allies' attack damage.

abilities.name: Triggerseed
abilities.howToUseIt: Shield an ally before a skirmish so it explodes into a slow and damage burst on whoever attacks them; if it never triggers it will refresh on its own.

abilities.name: Daisy!
abilities.howToUseIt: Summon Daisy before a teamfight to tank hits and threaten damage, then recast the ability to redirect her at a new target or location.

earlyGamePlan: Start in the jungle and route toward camps you can free quickly for experience, since you can't just auto-attack them down like other junglers. Once you have Rootcaller up, look to gank a lane by rooting the enemy laner so your ally can follow up, and drop Brushmaker patches near objectives you'll want to contest later.
quickTips: Plan your jungle clear around which camps you can grow and free fastest, since you can't fight them directly.
Save Rootcaller for setting up a kill rather than using it just to poke.
Drop Brushmaker in choke points before fights so you can ambush from brush.
Keep Triggerseed on an ally in lane - it costs you nothing if it never pops.
Never fight alone; you're built to peel and engage for teammates, not to duel.
Ward around your jungle since your early damage is too low to fight off invaders.
whatToExpect: An enemy Ivern will try to root you with Rootcaller so an ally can follow up, or spring an ambush from a Brushmaker patch he planted earlier. He deals little damage on his own, so punishing him directly is safe if he's isolated - but be ready for a surprise Daisy in bigger fights.

glossary.definition: When a jungler leaves their own area to ambush an enemy laner and help a teammate get a kill or lead.
A placeable item that reveals an area of the map so your team can see enemies coming.
Starting a fight by being the first to commit crowd control or damage onto the enemy team.
Using abilities to block, slow, or otherwise stop enemies from reaching a teammate you're protecting.
A fight involving most or all champions from both teams at once.
The route and order a jungler kills or, for Ivern, frees jungle camps to gain gold and experience.
Entering the enemy team's jungle, usually early in the game, to fight them or steal their camps.
